seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Semantic Kernel + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Semantic Kernel + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with Semantic Kernel enables real-time conversational AI by chaining speech-to-text, LLM reasoning with plugins and planners, and text-to-speech in a low-latency pipeline. Guardrails are essential due to the high-risk nature of live voice interactions where cascading plugin errors, hallucinated memories, or unsafe responses can damage user trust and safety. Vex integration as a kernel filter ensures response appropriateness, latency compliance, and conversation coherence without compromising sub-second performance.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Semantic Kernel
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents process audio streams through a real-time pipeline: speech-to-text (STT) transcribes input, Semantic Kernel's planner orchestrates plugins for reasoning/memory retrieval, and text-to-speech (TTS) synthesizes responses. Kernel functions handle plugin chaining while memory stores conversation context. Latency budgets demand <500ms end-to-end, making error detection critical to prevent stalls.

The Kernel acts as the orchestration layer, invoking functions like 'summarize_conversation' or 'search_memory' via planners. Vex filters intercept function results to validate outputs before TTS, catching issues like invalid plans or contaminated data. This architecture supports enterprise-scale deployments with Semantic Kernel's plugin ecosystem.

Real-time constraints require streaming STT/TTS and async kernel invocation, with Vex providing non-blocking verification.

risks.name: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
risks.description: Failed plugin calls (e.g., weather API timeout) cause planner to generate invalid subsequent function calls, stalling voice response.
risks.mitigation: Vex filter validates each function result before planner proceeds, with fallback responses.

risks.name: Planner generating invalid execution plans
risks.description: Planner creates nonsensical function sequences (e.g., 'search_memory' before 'transcribe_audio'), breaking conversation flow.
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic validation rejects malformed plans and triggers replanning within latency budget.

risks.name: Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
risks.description: Kernel memory retrieves fabricated conversation history, leading to contextually inappropriate responses.
risks.mitigation: Vex coherence checks cross-reference memory outputs against conversation state.

risks.name: Cross-plugin data contamination
risks.description: PII from one plugin (user profile) leaks into unrelated functions (public search), violating privacy.
risks.mitigation: Vex data isolation policies sanitize function inputs/outputs by plugin context.

implementation.title: Real-time STT to Kernel Pipeline (Python)
implementation.description: Stream audio to Whisper STT and pipe transcriptions directly into Semantic Kernel for planning.
implementation.code:
import asyncio
import semantic_kernel as sk
from semantic_kernel.connectors.ai.open_ai import OpenAIChatCompletion

kernel = sk.Kernel()
kernel.add_chat_service("gpt-4", OpenAIChatCompletion("gpt-4", api_key="your-key"))

async def voice_pipeline(audio_stream):
    # Streaming STT (Whisper)
    transcript = await stt_stream(audio_stream)
    
    # Kernel planning
    plan = kernel.create_semantic_plan("Process user request: " + transcript)
    result = await kernel.run_async(plan)
    
    return result

implementation.title: TypeScript Kernel with Streaming TTS
implementation.description: Async kernel invocation with streaming TTS output for sub-second latency.

implementation.title: Conversation Memory Integration (Python)
implementation.description: Persistent memory for multi-turn voice conversations with Semantic Kernel.
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel.memory import SemanticTextMemory

memory = SemanticTextMemory(storage=volatile_memory())

async def handle_turn(transcript: str, chat_history: list):
    # Store previous context
    for msg in chat_history:
        await memory.save_information_async(
            "chat_history", msg, "conversation"
        )
    
    # Retrieve relevant context
    context = await memory.search_async("conversation", transcript, limit=3)
    
    plan = kernel.create_semantic_plan(f"Context: {context}\nUser: {transcript}")
    return await kernel.run_async(plan)

vexIntegration.title: Python: Vex Kernel Result Filter
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as a post-function filter to validate all kernel outputs before TTS.

vexIntegration.title: TypeScript: Async Vex Plan Validation
vexIntegration.description: Validate planner-generated execution plans before execution to prevent cascading failures.

vexIntegration.title: Python: Memory Recall Verification
vexIntegration.description: Filter hallucinated memory retrievals to maintain conversation coherence.
vexIntegration.code:
async def verified_memory_search(query: str):
    # Raw memory retrieval
    raw_context = await memory.search_async("conversation", query, limit=3)
    
    # Vex coherence check
    coherence_check = vex.verify({
        "retrieved": raw_context,
        "query": query,
        "conversation_state": current_chat_history,
        "checks": ["temporal_consistency", "entity_match"]
    })
    
    if coherence_check.passed:
        return raw_context
    else:
        return "I don't have context for that yet."
